name: Rohan Kapoor
summary: Certified Ethical Hacker and SOC Analyst with 2 years of experience in VAPT, threat hunting, and incident response at an MSSP. Managed 300+ security incidents across BFSI and Healthcare clients. Active CTF player (Ranked Top 50 on HackTheBox India). Holds CEH, CompTIA Security+ and eJPT certifications. Deeply passionate about offensive security, red teaming, and MITRE ATT&CK threat modelling.

experience.title: Cyber Security Analyst (L2 SOC)
responsibilities: Monitored and triaged 10,000+ daily security alerts using Splunk SIEM, achieving MTTD of 9 minutes for critical P1 incidents.
Led 4 major incident response engagements involving ransomware (LockBit variant) and BEC attacks on BFSI clients.
Conducted 12 VAPT engagements (web + network) using Burp Suite, Nessus, and Metasploit; delivered formal pentest reports with CVSS-scored findings.
Developed 30+ custom Splunk detection rules reducing false positive rate from 18% to 6%.
Authored 8 threat intelligence reports on emerging APT groups targeting Indian financial sector, shared with CERT-In.
Mentored 3 L1 analysts through SOC onboarding and playbook training.

experience.title: Cybersecurity Intern
responsibilities: Assisted in ISO 27001 gap assessment for a mid-sized manufacturing client.
Performed manual vulnerability scanning using Nmap and Nessus Essentials.
Contributed to SOC runbook documentation for 15 alert categories.

projects.name: PhishGuard – Email Phishing Detection Tool
projects.description: Built an ML-powered phishing email classifier achieving 96.3% accuracy on 10,000-email dataset. Deployed as internal tool at CyberSafe reducing SOC analyst phishing triage time by 40%.

projects.name: SplunkHunter – Automated Threat Hunting Scripts
projects.description: Collection of 25+ automated threat hunting queries and scripts mapped to MITRE ATT&CK techniques. Used internally at CyberSafe and shared on GitHub with 400+ stars.

achievements: Top 50 Ranked – HackTheBox India (Hall of Fame entry)
1st Place – CyberSafe Internal Red vs Blue Team Exercise (2023)
Published CVE-2023-XXXXX: Discovered XSS vulnerability in open-source SIEM plugin (responsibly disclosed)
Manipal Institute Gold Medal – Cybersecurity Specialization (Batch 2022)
